QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_30

question.text: Rajesh submits a mutual fund purchase application from a B-30 city. Which of the following statements about additional TER is correct?

answer.text: No additional TER is permitted for B-30 cities under current regulations
Additional TER applies only if the investor is a first-time mutual fund investor
The AMC can charge additional TER of up to 0.30% for inflows from B-30 cities, applicable only for retail transactions up to Rs.2 lakh
Additional TER of 0.50% applies to all B-30 transactions regardless of amount

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI allows AMCs to charge additional TER for inflows from B-30 (Beyond top 30) cities to incentivize geographical expansion. This applies only to retail transactions (up to Rs.2 lakh per transaction). **The Trap:** The B-30 additional TER is not unlimited -- it applies only to retail-sized transactions. Large institutional transactions from B-30 cities do not qualify for additional TER. **Cross-Exam Context:** T-30/B-30 classification is based on investor PIN codes. NISM Series V-A covers this geographical incentive structure that encourages mutual fund penetration in smaller cities. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If you operate in B-30 cities, this is your competitive advantage. AMCs are motivated to push distribution in these areas because they can charge slightly higher TER, which partly funds higher distributor commissions for B-30 sourcing.
